<system-reminder>
The user named this session "lab-implementation". This may indicate the session's focus or intent.
</system-reminder>

Let's work on: /workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/handoff-laboratory-implementation.md

I will be away from keyboard, so work unattended. Do as much implementation as possible. Skipping unworkable items if necessary and possible giving ordering constraints. If there are questions with reasonable default answers, move forward with those answers and present a list of decisions needing review at the end so we can determine if adjustements/rework are needed. Stop only when all remaining tasks are unworkable, or you reach the point of needing to complete a real world trial. The `vibers` guild is not running, but feel free to start/stop the daemon to reload it if any live verification is needed.

1	# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing
2	
3	The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
4	skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs and a resolved
5	archive design. What's left is the probe registry, the three
6	standard probes, the archive engine, the new CLI tools, the
7	codified smoke test, infra-spec retirement, documentation, and
8	the first real-world trial port. The archive-design conversation
9	has been resolved (see click `c-momaa5o9` and
10	`packages/laboratory/README.md`); this handoff is purely
11	incremental implementation against that specification.
12	
13	## Where we are
14	
15	Ten commits past the apparatus skeleton:
16	
17	  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
18	  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
19	  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
20	  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
21	  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
22	  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
23	  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
24	  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
25	  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs
26	  082ad189  archive design — DB-authoritative + extract tool
27	  34b328ae  archive design — atomicity tightening
28	
29	Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:
30	
31	  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
32	      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
33	      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
34	      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.
35	
36	  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
37	      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
38	      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
39	      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
40	      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.
41	
42	  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
43	    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
44	      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
45	      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
46	      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
47	      multi-step scenarios.
48	
49	Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts injects `_trial:{slug, writId,
50	fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens — general
51	improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all fixture
52	engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.
53	
54	123/123 unit tests passing. Two trials posted live against
55	vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines each, ~14s
56	wallclock).
57	
58	The archive design conversation also wrapped (`c-momaa5o9`
59	concluded). High-level shape, with the full spec at
60	`packages/laboratory/README.md` → "Archive design":
61	
62	  - **DB-authoritative.** Captured trial data lives in the lab
63	    guild's stacks DB, not the sanctum filesystem.
64	  - **Three books.** `lab-trial-archives` (archive-engine-owned
65	    index, one row per trial); `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` (probe-
66	    stacks-dump-owned, generic JSON-bodied rows from test-guild
67	    books); `lab-trial-codex-commits` (probe-git-range-owned,
68	    one row per commit with 10MB diff cap).
69	  - **Probes own their own books.** Archive engine has no
70	    schema opinions about probe data — it just records which
71	    probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.
72	  - **FK linkage only.** `lab-trial-archives.trialId` →
73	    `clerk/writs.id`. No clerk link kind (clerk links are
74	    writ-to-writ; archive records aren't writs).
75	  - **On-demand filesystem materialization.** `nsg lab trial-
76	    extract <trialId> --to <dir>` reads from books and writes
77	    files; probe registry dispatches per-probe `extract()`
78	    handlers.
79	
80	## Suggested order
81	
82	Registry first (gates the probe interface and the extract
83	tool), then the three probes in parallel (each registers with
84	the registry), then the archive engine (consumes probe
85	upstream yields), then the CLI tools, then cleanup tail. One
86	commit per engine pair as before.
87	
88	### 1. Probe registry + extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`) — MVP-required
89	
90	The seam that lets `nsg lab trial-extract` be probe-pluggable
91	without hardcoded knowledge. Probe engines self-declare an
92	`extract(trialId, targetDir, guild) → { files: [...] }`
93	handler; the laboratory plugin builds a probe registry from
94	per-probe registrations on plugin start; the extract tool
95	dispatches by `engineId` via the registry.
96	
97	This is the first piece because it shapes the probe-engine
98	interface (every probe lands on top of it) and gates the
99	extract tool. Small but load-bearing.
100	
101	### 2. Three standard probes (`c-momaa3w7`)
102	
103	Each probe lives under `src/probes/<name>/{engine.ts, book.ts,
104	extractor.ts}` — per-probe directory keeps the v2 "lift into
105	separate plugin" path mechanical. Each registers with the
106	probe registry on plugin start.
107	
108	  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — walks every book in the test
109	    guild, writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-
110	    dumps`. Summary: `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N } }`.
111	    Test guild discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream`
112	    (any yield with `{guildName, guildPath}`), same pattern as
113	    scenario-xguild.
114	
115	  - **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between codex
116	    base and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to `lab-trial-
117	    codex-commits`. Summary: `{ headSha, commitCount,
118	    totalDiffBytes }`. **Fails loud on any single diff > 10MB**
119	    (tripwire, not a constraint we expect to hit). Codex
120	    discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream` (any yield
121	    with `{codexName, ...}`); read SHAs and the bare-repo path
122	    from whatever the codex-fixture yields.
123	
124	  - **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig
125	    template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins,
126	    manifest snapshot. Summary-as-data; no bulk book. Reads
127	    rig metadata from `context.rigId`, manifest from the trial
128	    writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. Greenfield (no stub yet —
129	    add it during this work). Should be included in the
130	    default rig template.
131	
132	Probes can develop in parallel — they're independent of each
133	other. Stacks-dump and git-range replace existing stubs in
134	`stubs.ts`; trial-context is brand new.
135	
136	### 3. Archive engine (`c-momkqtn5`)
137	
138	`lab.archive` — reads probe summaries from `context.upstream`
139	and writes one atomic row to `lab-trial-archives` with
140	`{id, trialId, archivedAt, probes: [{id, engineId, summary}]}`.
141	**Per-engine atomicity** (the rig grafts probes before archive
142	as separate engines, so each engine is its own transaction;
143	archive row writes once on success). Trials whose rigs fail
144	before reaching archive simply have no matching row;
145	analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward, so
146	orphan probe rows don't surface.
147	
148	The teardown engines' archive-presence safety check tightens:
149	**from** "context.upstream.archive defined" **to** "archive row
150	exists for this trialId" — query `lab-trial-archives WHERE
151	trialId=?` and require non-empty.
152	
153	### 4. CLI tools (folds into `c-momkil4p` for `trial-extract`; new tools for show/export-book)
154	
155	Three new tools, all with the `lab-` prefix:
156	
157	  - `lab-trial-show <trialId>` — print archive metadata + probe
158	    summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
159	  - `lab-trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]` —
160	    materialize captured data to a directory; refuses to
161	    overwrite without `--force`. Probe registry dispatches
162	    per-probe extractors.
163	  - `lab-trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format
164	    jsonl|json]` — stream one source book for analysis pipelines.
165	    Default `jsonl`.
166	
167	Once a second `lab-` tool ships, the framework's auto-grouping
168	activates (≥2 tools sharing a prefix) and the CLI surfaces
169	them all under `nsg lab <subcommand>` — including the existing
170	`lab-trial-post` becoming `nsg lab trial-post`. This is a
171	presentation change driven by the framework, not anything the
172	laboratory plugin controls.
173	
174	### 5. Codified smoke test (`c-momaa75l`)
175	
176	The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` worked
177	end-to-end and are useful as templates. Codify them as a
178	recurring package test: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
179	trivial trial via the CLI, assert the rig completes with every
180	expected engine in the expected order, **assert the archive
181	row + probe data exist**, clean up. Look at spider's
182	integration tests for precedent
183	(`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).
184	
185	The smoke test should exercise the full pipeline (fixtures →
186	scenario → probes → archive → teardowns), not just the bits
187	that previous unit tests covered.
188	
189	### 6. Retire the superseded infra spec (`c-momaa8mk`)
190	
191	`experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is what the
192	apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive vs delete-since-
193	superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the click that tracked the
194	spec as a P3 prerequisite) with a pointer to the apparatus.
195	
196	### 7. Documentation (`c-momaaa3t`)
197	
198	  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — already carries the
199	    architecture overview and archive design. Flesh out manifest
200	    authoring, the rig flow narrative, and end-user examples.
201	  - Apparatus doc under `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/`
202	    describing the apparatus's place in the architecture.
203	  - User-facing guide "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab
204	    trials" — sanctum-side, since end-users consume sanctum
205	    docs.
206	
207	### 8. Port the first real-world trial (`c-momaab8y`)
208	
209	Pick the next P3 experiment — likely X016 orientation
210	suppression (see cost-optimization landscape at click
211	`c-mok4nke6` for the candidate list). Author its manifest, run
212	it, verify findings land in `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` and
213	`lab-trial-codex-commits`, extract to a directory, run analysis.
214	
215	This is the capstone — validates the apparatus on real research
216	work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.
217	Likely surfaces missed assumptions in the archive layout or
218	probe outputs that a synthetic smoke test won't catch; expect
219	a small iteration loop on the first run.
220	
221	## Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)
222	
223	Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
224	vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking the
225	steps above:
226	
227	  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
228	    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
229	    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
230	    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
231	    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
232	    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
233	    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
234	    chain succeeded).
235	
236	  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
237	    from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates guild.json,
238	    but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium state isn't
239	    notified. Daemon's next guild.json write reverts the
240	    orphans. Vibers currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
241	    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
242	    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic (orphan bare repos
243	    don't run code), but means CLI-side cleanup doesn't stick.
244	
245	## Implementation discipline
246	
247	  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
248	    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
249	    implementation file under `engines/` (or `src/probes/<name>/`
250	    for probes). Conclude the matching click in the same commit.
251	  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
252	    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
253	    optional `retry`. Probes additionally provide `extract()`
254	    per the registry contract. Errors throw; the spider routes
255	    to engine failure.
256	  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
257	    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
258	    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
259	    `ext.laboratory.config`.
260	  - **Use injected `_trial` context for trial-aware defaulting.**
261	    phases.ts populates it; engines read it for sensible
262	    name/path defaults. Codex and guild fixtures already
263	    establish the pattern.
264	  - **Use upstream duck-typing for cross-engine wiring.** No
265	    manifest-level "this is the codex / test guild" declarations
266	    — engines walk `context.upstream` looking for yields that
267	    match the shape they need. Codex discovery, test-guild
268	    discovery, and archive's probe-summary discovery all follow
269	    this pattern.
270	  - **Atomicity is per-engine, not per-trial.** Don't try to
271	    span SQLite transactions across engines (the rig structure
272	    forbids it — probes graft as separate engines ahead of
273	    archive). Each probe writes its data atomically per-probe;
274	    the archive engine writes its index row atomically per-row;
275	    orphan probe rows from rig-failed trials are tolerated
276	    (analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward,
277	    so orphans don't surface).
278	  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing, post
279	    a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes. The
280	    smoke-test pattern (write tmp manifest, `nsg lab-trial-post`,
281	    poll `nsg writ show --format json` until classification ===
282	    'terminal') works for one-off verification before the
283	    codified test lands.
284	
285	## Pointers to read first
286	
287	  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — **canonical archive design
288	    spec.** Architecture, book schemas, atomicity, registry
289	    contract, CLI surface. The single source of truth for the
290	    archive subsystem.
291	  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
292	    click subtree.
293	  - `nsg click show c-momaa5o9` — archive design conclusion (the
294	    reasoning trail behind the README spec).
295	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
296	    orchestrators that route into each engine; documents the
297	    `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every grafted work engine
298	    sees.
299	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
300	    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
301	    rollback, archive-presence safety check, `_trial`-driven
302	    defaulting).
303	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
304	    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
305	    plugin-install loop and duck-typed codex discovery from
306	    upstream.
307	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
308	    pattern for engines that shell out, parse JSON, optionally
309	    poll until classification-terminal.
310	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still a
311	    stub. `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`, and
312	    `lab.probe-git-range` are here. `lab.probe-trial-context`
313	    is greenfield (no stub yet — add it during probe work).
314	  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests used
315	    in live testing; useful templates for the codified smoke
316	    test.
317	
318	## Live test environment
319	
320	Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. After plugin code
321	changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
322	registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
323	per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
324	loaded its engines at startup.
325	
326	**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from the
327	previous live-verify session that survived a daemon write.
328	They won't run anything but force `--codex` selection on
329	`lab-trial-post` invocations until cleared via daemon restart.
330	
331	For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
332	natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
333	isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
334	guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
335	`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the test
336	guilds (which default to npm).
337

# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing

The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs and a resolved
archive design. What's left is the probe registry, the three
standard probes, the archive engine, the new CLI tools, the
codified smoke test, infra-spec retirement, documentation, and
the first real-world trial port. The archive-design conversation
has been resolved (see click `c-momaa5o9` and
`packages/laboratory/README.md`); this handoff is purely
incremental implementation against that specification.

## Where we are

Ten commits past the apparatus skeleton:

  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs
  082ad189  archive design — DB-authoritative + extract tool
  34b328ae  archive design — atomicity tightening

Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:

  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.

  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.

  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
      multi-step scenarios.

Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts injects `_trial:{slug, writId,
fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens — general
improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all fixture
engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.

123/123 unit tests passing. Two trials posted live against
vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines each, ~14s
wallclock).

The archive design conversation also wrapped (`c-momaa5o9`
concluded). High-level shape, with the full spec at
`packages/laboratory/README.md` → "Archive design":

  - **DB-authoritative.** Captured trial data lives in the lab
    guild's stacks DB, not the sanctum filesystem.
  - **Three books.** `lab-trial-archives` (archive-engine-owned
    index, one row per trial); `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` (probe-
    stacks-dump-owned, generic JSON-bodied rows from test-guild
    books); `lab-trial-codex-commits` (probe-git-range-owned,
    one row per commit with 10MB diff cap).
  - **Probes own their own books.** Archive engine has no
    schema opinions about probe data — it just records which
    probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.
  - **FK linkage only.** `lab-trial-archives.trialId` →
    `clerk/writs.id`. No clerk link kind (clerk links are
    writ-to-writ; archive records aren't writs).
  - **On-demand filesystem materialization.** `nsg lab trial-
    extract <trialId> --to <dir>` reads from books and writes
    files; probe registry dispatches per-probe `extract()`
    handlers.

## Suggested order

Registry first (gates the probe interface and the extract
tool), then the three probes in parallel (each registers with
the registry), then the archive engine (consumes probe
upstream yields), then the CLI tools, then cleanup tail. One
commit per engine pair as before.

### 1. Probe registry + extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`) — MVP-required

The seam that lets `nsg lab trial-extract` be probe-pluggable
without hardcoded knowledge. Probe engines self-declare an
`extract(trialId, targetDir, guild) → { files: [...] }`
handler; the laboratory plugin builds a probe registry from
per-probe registrations on plugin start; the extract tool
dispatches by `engineId` via the registry.

This is the first piece because it shapes the probe-engine
interface (every probe lands on top of it) and gates the
extract tool. Small but load-bearing.

### 2. Three standard probes (`c-momaa3w7`)

Each probe lives under `src/probes/<name>/{engine.ts, book.ts,
extractor.ts}` — per-probe directory keeps the v2 "lift into
separate plugin" path mechanical. Each registers with the
probe registry on plugin start.

  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — walks every book in the test
    guild, writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-
    dumps`. Summary: `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N } }`.
    Test guild discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream`
    (any yield with `{guildName, guildPath}`), same pattern as
    scenario-xguild.

  - **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between codex
    base and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to `lab-trial-
    codex-commits`. Summary: `{ headSha, commitCount,
    totalDiffBytes }`. **Fails loud on any single diff > 10MB**
    (tripwire, not a constraint we expect to hit). Codex
    discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream` (any yield
    with `{codexName, ...}`); read SHAs and the bare-repo path
    from whatever the codex-fixture yields.

  - **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig
    template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins,
    manifest snapshot. Summary-as-data; no bulk book. Reads
    rig metadata from `context.rigId`, manifest from the trial
    writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. Greenfield (no stub yet —
    add it during this work). Should be included in the
    default rig template.

Probes can develop in parallel — they're independent of each
other. Stacks-dump and git-range replace existing stubs in
`stubs.ts`; trial-context is brand new.

### 3. Archive engine (`c-momkqtn5`)

`lab.archive` — reads probe summaries from `context.upstream`
and writes one atomic row to `lab-trial-archives` with
`{id, trialId, archivedAt, probes: [{id, engineId, summary}]}`.
**Per-engine atomicity** (the rig grafts probes before archive
as separate engines, so each engine is its own transaction;
archive row writes once on success). Trials whose rigs fail
before reaching archive simply have no matching row;
analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward, so
orphan probe rows don't surface.

The teardown engines' archive-presence safety check tightens:
**from** "context.upstream.archive defined" **to** "archive row
exists for this trialId" — query `lab-trial-archives WHERE
trialId=?` and require non-empty.

### 4. CLI tools (folds into `c-momkil4p` for `trial-extract`; new tools for show/export-book)

Three new tools, all with the `lab-` prefix:

  - `lab-trial-show <trialId>` — print archive metadata + probe
    summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
  - `lab-trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]` —
    materialize captured data to a directory; refuses to
    overwrite without `--force`. Probe registry dispatches
    per-probe extractors.
  - `lab-trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format
    jsonl|json]` — stream one source book for analysis pipelines.
    Default `jsonl`.

Once a second `lab-` tool ships, the framework's auto-grouping
activates (≥2 tools sharing a prefix) and the CLI surfaces
them all under `nsg lab <subcommand>` — including the existing
`lab-trial-post` becoming `nsg lab trial-post`. This is a
presentation change driven by the framework, not anything the
laboratory plugin controls.

### 5. Codified smoke test (`c-momaa75l`)

The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` worked
end-to-end and are useful as templates. Codify them as a
recurring package test: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
trivial trial via the CLI, assert the rig completes with every
expected engine in the expected order, **assert the archive
row + probe data exist**, clean up. Look at spider's
integration tests for precedent
(`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).

The smoke test should exercise the full pipeline (fixtures →
scenario → probes → archive → teardowns), not just the bits
that previous unit tests covered.

### 6. Retire the superseded infra spec (`c-momaa8mk`)

`experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is what the
apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive vs delete-since-
superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the click that tracked the
spec as a P3 prerequisite) with a pointer to the apparatus.

### 7. Documentation (`c-momaaa3t`)

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — already carries the
    architecture overview and archive design. Flesh out manifest
    authoring, the rig flow narrative, and end-user examples.
  - Apparatus doc under `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/`
    describing the apparatus's place in the architecture.
  - User-facing guide "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab
    trials" — sanctum-side, since end-users consume sanctum
    docs.

### 8. Port the first real-world trial (`c-momaab8y`)

Pick the next P3 experiment — likely X016 orientation
suppression (see cost-optimization landscape at click
`c-mok4nke6` for the candidate list). Author its manifest, run
it, verify findings land in `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` and
`lab-trial-codex-commits`, extract to a directory, run analysis.

This is the capstone — validates the apparatus on real research
work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.
Likely surfaces missed assumptions in the archive layout or
probe outputs that a synthetic smoke test won't catch; expect
a small iteration loop on the first run.

## Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)

Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking the
steps above:

  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
    chain succeeded).

  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
    from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates guild.json,
    but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium state isn't
    notified. Daemon's next guild.json write reverts the
    orphans. Vibers currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic (orphan bare repos
    don't run code), but means CLI-side cleanup doesn't stick.

## Implementation discipline

  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
    implementation file under `engines/` (or `src/probes/<name>/`
    for probes). Conclude the matching click in the same commit.
  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
    optional `retry`. Probes additionally provide `extract()`
    per the registry contract. Errors throw; the spider routes
    to engine failure.
  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
    `ext.laboratory.config`.
  - **Use injected `_trial` context for trial-aware defaulting.**
    phases.ts populates it; engines read it for sensible
    name/path defaults. Codex and guild fixtures already
    establish the pattern.
  - **Use upstream duck-typing for cross-engine wiring.** No
    manifest-level "this is the codex / test guild" declarations
    — engines walk `context.upstream` looking for yields that
    match the shape they need. Codex discovery, test-guild
    discovery, and archive's probe-summary discovery all follow
    this pattern.
  - **Atomicity is per-engine, not per-trial.** Don't try to
    span SQLite transactions across engines (the rig structure
    forbids it — probes graft as separate engines ahead of
    archive). Each probe writes its data atomically per-probe;
    the archive engine writes its index row atomically per-row;
    orphan probe rows from rig-failed trials are tolerated
    (analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward,
    so orphans don't surface).
  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing, post
    a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes. The
    smoke-test pattern (write tmp manifest, `nsg lab-trial-post`,
    poll `nsg writ show --format json` until classification ===
    'terminal') works for one-off verification before the
    codified test lands.

## Pointers to read first

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — **canonical archive design
    spec.** Architecture, book schemas, atomicity, registry
    contract, CLI surface. The single source of truth for the
    archive subsystem.
  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
    click subtree.
  - `nsg click show c-momaa5o9` — archive design conclusion (the
    reasoning trail behind the README spec).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
    orchestrators that route into each engine; documents the
    `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every grafted work engine
    sees.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
    rollback, archive-presence safety check, `_trial`-driven
    defaulting).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
    plugin-install loop and duck-typed codex discovery from
    upstream.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out, parse JSON, optionally
    poll until classification-terminal.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still a
    stub. `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`, and
    `lab.probe-git-range` are here. `lab.probe-trial-context`
    is greenfield (no stub yet — add it during probe work).
  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests used
    in live testing; useful templates for the codified smoke
    test.

## Live test environment

Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. After plugin code
changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
loaded its engines at startup.

**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from the
previous live-verify session that survived a daemon write.
They won't run anything but force `--codex` selection on
`lab-trial-post` invocations until cleared via daemon restart.

For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the test
guilds (which default to npm).

1	# The Laboratory
2	
3	Apparatus for running trial-shaped experiments on guild configurations.
4	
5	## Audiences
6	
7	- **Nexus dev** — cost/quality tuning, prompt evaluation, plugin variant
8	  comparison. Replaces the standalone-bash spec at
9	  `experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md`.
10	- **End users** — evaluate prompts, plugins, and config variants by
11	  authoring trial manifests against a stable apparatus surface.
12	
13	## Architecture (MVP0)
14	
15	- **Writ type:** `trial` — a single execution unit. Lifecycle mirrors
16	  mandate (`new → open → completed | failed | cancelled`, with `stuck`
17	  as a non-terminal off `open`). Trials are leaves in v1; the
18	  higher-level `experiment` grouping is parked for v2.
19	- **Rig template:** `post-and-collect-default` — composes
20	  fixture-setup, scenario, probe, teardown, and archive engines from
21	  the writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. One canonical template; extension
22	  is via plugin contributions, not in-template slots.
23	- **Engines:**
24	  - **Fixtures** — set up and tear down disposable surfaces (codex
25	    repos, test guilds). Form a dep DAG; topo-sorted at template
26	    instantiation.
27	  - **Scenario** — the workload. v1 uses cross-guild commission-post +
28	    wait-for-writ-terminal as the canonical scenario engine pair.
29	  - **Probes** — extract data from the trial. Own their own books for
30	    bulk data and yield a summary that lands in the archive index.
31	  - **Archive** — writes the per-trial index row and orchestrates the
32	    probe set. See **Archive design** below.
33	- **Authoring:** YAML manifest via `nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>`.
34	  Manifest shape mirrors `ext.laboratory.config` exactly.
35	
36	## Archive design
37	
38	The archive subsystem is **DB-authoritative with on-demand filesystem
39	materialization.** Captured trial data lives in the lab guild's stacks
40	DB; the filesystem story is provided by an extract tool that reads from
41	the DB on demand. This avoids the brittleness of long-lived references
42	between the lab guild's books and patron-owned filesystem trees.
43	
44	Resolved at click `c-momaa5o9`.
45	
46	### Books
47	
48	Three books participate. The archive engine owns one (`lab-trial-archives`,
49	the index); the two built-in probes each own one (their captured data).
50	
51	#### `lab-trial-archives` — owned by the archive engine
52	
53	One row per archived trial. Tiny. Written-once at archive time. The
54	archive engine has **no schema opinions about probe data** — its job is
55	to record what probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.
56	
57	```ts
58	interface LabTrialArchive {
59	  id: string;                            // generated
60	  trialId: string;                       // FK → clerk/writs (indexed)
61	  archivedAt: string;                    // ISO
62	
63	  probes: Array<{
64	    id: string;                          // probe id from trial config
65	    engineId: string;                    // e.g. 'lab.probe-stacks-dump'
66	    summary: Record<string, unknown>;    // opaque to archive engine
67	  }>;
68	}
69	```
70	
71	Row existence is the success signal — there is no in-progress / failed
72	row state. The archive engine writes the row atomically on success;
73	trials whose rigs failed before reaching archive simply have no
74	matching row.
75	
76	Trial-level facts (manifest body, codex base SHA, codex upstream URL,
77	plugin specifications) are **not duplicated here** — they live on the
78	trial writ at `ext.laboratory.config`. Reproducibility-relevant runtime
79	facts (resolved plugin pins, framework SHA, rig template name) are
80	captured by `lab.probe-trial-context` and live in that probe's summary.
81	
82	#### `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` — owned by `lab.probe-stacks-dump`
83	
84	One row per source-row, across every book in the test guild. Generic
85	JSON-bodied; querying is via SQLite JSON1 expressions.
86	
87	```ts
88	interface LabTrialStacksDump {
89	  id: string;                            // generated
90	  trialId: string;                       // FK (indexed)
91	  sourceBook: string;                    // (trialId, sourceBook) indexed
92	  sourceRowId: string;
93	  capturedAt: string;
94	  body: Record<string, unknown>;          // the source row, verbatim
95	}
96	```
97	
98	Indexes added per hot query — e.g.
99	`CREATE INDEX … ON … (trialId, json_extract(body, '$.cost'))
100	 WHERE sourceBook='animator/sessions'`.
101	
102	#### `lab-trial-codex-commits` — owned by `lab.probe-git-range`
103	
104	One row per captured codex commit. Body is the diff text.
105	
106	```ts
107	interface LabTrialCodexCommit {
108	  id: string;
109	  trialId: string;                       // FK (indexed)
110	  sequence: number;                      // ordinal within trial
111	  sha: string;                           // 40-char
112	  message: string;
113	  filesChanged: number;
114	  insertions: number;
115	  deletions: number;
116	  diff: string;                          // patch text
117	}
118	```
119	
120	**Big-diff tripwire:** the archive engine fails loud if any single diff
121	exceeds 10MB. Realistic diffs are <500KB; the cap is a "we'll figure
122	out blob storage if it ever bites" tripwire, not a constraint we expect
123	to hit.
124	
125	### Standard probes
126	
127	Three probes ship in MVP, registered with the laboratory plugin's probe
128	registry:
129	
130	- **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — reads every book in the test guild,
131	  writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-dumps`. Summary
132	  is `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N, ... } }`.
133	- **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between the codex base
134	  and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to
135	  `lab-trial-codex-commits`. Summary is
136	  `{ headSha, commitCount, totalDiffBytes }`.
137	- **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig template name,
138	  framework SHA, resolved plugin pins, and a snapshot of the trial
139	  manifest. Summary-as-data: no bulk book; the summary itself is the
140	  captured data. Included in the default rig template; opt out by
141	  authoring a custom template.
142	
143	### Trial-writ linkage
144	
145	Just the foreign key. `lab-trial-archives.trialId` references
146	`clerk/writs.id`. Lookup is via FK query — there is no
147	`laboratory.archived-as` clerk link, because clerk links are
148	writ-to-writ and archive records are not writs.
149	
150	`nsg writ show <trialId>` shows the trial writ with its config. To
151	surface the archive, run `nsg lab trial-show <trialId>`.
152	
153	### Atomicity
154	
155	Per-engine, not per-trial. The rig grafts probes ahead of the archive
156	engine; each probe writes its data atomically inside its own SQLite
157	transaction. The archive engine runs after all probes complete and
158	writes the `lab-trial-archives` index row atomically once. The index
159	row is the join key for queries — probe-book rows without a matching
160	archive-index row are orphan data from trials whose rigs failed before
161	reaching the archive step. Orphans are safe to ignore in queries
162	(every analytical query starts from `lab-trial-archives` and joins
163	out). Cleanup of orphan probe rows is future polish; MVP leaves them
164	in place.
165	
166	The teardown engines (`lab.guild-teardown`, `lab.codex-teardown`)
167	refuse to run unless the trial's archive row exists.
168	
169	### Probe registry and extraction-dispatch
170	
171	Probes self-declare an extraction handler that materializes their
172	captured data to a directory. The extract tool dispatches via the
173	registry — no hardcoded probe knowledge in the tool itself. MVP
174	contract:
175	
176	```ts
177	interface ProbeEngineDesign extends EngineDesign {
178	  // existing
179	  run(givens, context): Promise<EngineRunResult>;
180	
181	  // new
182	  extract(args: {
183	    trialId: string;
184	    targetDir: string;        // probe writes outputs here (subdir)
185	    guild: GuildHandle;        // for reading the probe's books
186	  }): Promise<{
187	    files: Array<{ path: string; bytes: number }>;
188	  }>;
189	}
190	```
191	
192	Tracked at click `c-momkil4p`. Each built-in probe ships its own
193	extractor:
194	
195	- `lab.probe-stacks-dump` materializes to
196	  `<targetDir>/stacks-export/<plugin>-<book>.json` (one JSON array
197	  per source book).
198	- `lab.probe-git-range` materializes to
199	  `<targetDir>/codex-history/{commits-manifest.yaml,NNNN-<sha>.patch}`.
200	- `lab.probe-trial-context` materializes to
201	  `<targetDir>/trial-context.yaml`.
202	
203	The extract tool composes per-probe outputs and additionally generates
204	`manifest.yaml` (from the trial writ's `ext.laboratory.config`) and
205	`README.md` (from archive metadata + probe summaries) at the top
206	level.
207	
208	### CLI surface
209	
210	- **`nsg lab trial-show <trialId>`** — print archive metadata + probe
211	  summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
212	- **`nsg lab trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]`** —
213	  materialize all captured data to a directory. Refuses to overwrite
214	  unless `--force`. Probe registry dispatches per-probe extractors.
215	- **`nsg lab trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format jsonl|json]`** —
216	  stream one source book for analysis pipelines. Default `jsonl`.
217	
218	For programmatic analysis without going through extract: scripts can
219	attach the lab guild's stacks DB directly (DuckDB reads SQLite
220	natively) and query `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` / `lab-trial-codex-commits`
221	with JSON1 expressions.
222	
223	### Annotations live sanctum-side
224	
225	Analysis notes, findings, and human-authored interpretation of trial
226	data live in sanctum markdown that references `trialId` as the join
227	key — not in any laboratory book. The captured data in books is
228	immutable apparatus output; analysis is a sanctum activity. They were
229	never the same thing.
230	
231	### Plugin packaging
232	
233	For MVP, all three books and all three standard probes bundle in
234	`@shardworks/laboratory`. The code organizes per-probe
235	(`src/probes/<probe-name>/{engine.ts, book.ts, extractor.ts}`) and the
236	probe registry is built from per-probe registrations rather than a
237	hardcoded list. When a third-party probe forces the issue, lifting a
238	built-in probe into its own plugin (`@shardworks/lab-probe-stacks-dump`,
239	etc.) is a mechanical move — no architectural surgery. Tracked as a
240	parked v2 path; no click filed yet pending a forcing function.
241	
242	## Status
243	
244	Skeleton with stub engines. The trial writ type is registered; the rig
245	template, manifest CLI, and stub engines are wired end-to-end (smoke
246	tested against `vibers`). The remaining MVP work: real engine
247	implementations under click `c-moma9llq` — codex fixtures, guild
248	fixtures, scenario engines, probe engines (now including
249	`lab.probe-trial-context`), the archive engine, and the probe registry
250	+ extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`).
251	
252	## Background
253	
254	This package previously held a CDC-based observational stub (data
255	mirroring of writs/sessions into the sanctum). That instrument was
256	retired 2026-04-30 — its underlying signals (patron-set spec quality
257	ratings, structured commission review) had been hollowed out by the
258	shift to automated planning and static review pipelines. The package
259	was kept as a no-op so existing `guild.json` registrations stayed
260	loadable; the apparatus reshape reuses the package and the registered
261	plugin id, but the old data-mirroring code and types are gone.
262

# The Laboratory

Apparatus for running trial-shaped experiments on guild configurations.

## Audiences

- **Nexus dev** — cost/quality tuning, prompt evaluation, plugin variant
  comparison. Replaces the standalone-bash spec at
  `experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md`.
- **End users** — evaluate prompts, plugins, and config variants by
  authoring trial manifests against a stable apparatus surface.

## Architecture (MVP0)

- **Writ type:** `trial` — a single execution unit. Lifecycle mirrors
  mandate (`new → open → completed | failed | cancelled`, with `stuck`
  as a non-terminal off `open`). Trials are leaves in v1; the
  higher-level `experiment` grouping is parked for v2.
- **Rig template:** `post-and-collect-default` — composes
  fixture-setup, scenario, probe, teardown, and archive engines from
  the writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. One canonical template; extension
  is via plugin contributions, not in-template slots.
- **Engines:**
  - **Fixtures** — set up and tear down disposable surfaces (codex
    repos, test guilds). Form a dep DAG; topo-sorted at template
    instantiation.
  - **Scenario** — the workload. v1 uses cross-guild commission-post +
    wait-for-writ-terminal as the canonical scenario engine pair.
  - **Probes** — extract data from the trial. Own their own books for
    bulk data and yield a summary that lands in the archive index.
  - **Archive** — writes the per-trial index row and orchestrates the
    probe set. See **Archive design** below.
- **Authoring:** YAML manifest via `nsg lab trial post --manifest <file>`.
  Manifest shape mirrors `ext.laboratory.config` exactly.

## Archive design

The archive subsystem is **DB-authoritative with on-demand filesystem
materialization.** Captured trial data lives in the lab guild's stacks
DB; the filesystem story is provided by an extract tool that reads from
the DB on demand. This avoids the brittleness of long-lived references
between the lab guild's books and patron-owned filesystem trees.

Resolved at click `c-momaa5o9`.

### Books

Three books participate. The archive engine owns one (`lab-trial-archives`,
the index); the two built-in probes each own one (their captured data).

#### `lab-trial-archives` — owned by the archive engine

One row per archived trial. Tiny. Written-once at archive time. The
archive engine has **no schema opinions about probe data** — its job is
to record what probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.

```ts
interface LabTrialArchive {
  id: string;                            // generated
  trialId: string;                       // FK → clerk/writs (indexed)
  archivedAt: string;                    // ISO

  probes: Array<{
    id: string;                          // probe id from trial config
    engineId: string;                    // e.g. 'lab.probe-stacks-dump'
    summary: Record<string, unknown>;    // opaque to archive engine
  }>;
}
```

Row existence is the success signal — there is no in-progress / failed
row state. The archive engine writes the row atomically on success;
trials whose rigs failed before reaching archive simply have no
matching row.

Trial-level facts (manifest body, codex base SHA, codex upstream URL,
plugin specifications) are **not duplicated here** — they live on the
trial writ at `ext.laboratory.config`. Reproducibility-relevant runtime
facts (resolved plugin pins, framework SHA, rig template name) are
captured by `lab.probe-trial-context` and live in that probe's summary.

#### `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` — owned by `lab.probe-stacks-dump`

One row per source-row, across every book in the test guild. Generic
JSON-bodied; querying is via SQLite JSON1 expressions.

```ts
interface LabTrialStacksDump {
  id: string;                            // generated
  trialId: string;                       // FK (indexed)
  sourceBook: string;                    // (trialId, sourceBook) indexed
  sourceRowId: string;
  capturedAt: string;
  body: Record<string, unknown>;          // the source row, verbatim
}
```

Indexes added per hot query — e.g.
`CREATE INDEX … ON … (trialId, json_extract(body, '$.cost'))
 WHERE sourceBook='animator/sessions'`.

#### `lab-trial-codex-commits` — owned by `lab.probe-git-range`

One row per captured codex commit. Body is the diff text.

```ts
interface LabTrialCodexCommit {
  id: string;
  trialId: string;                       // FK (indexed)
  sequence: number;                      // ordinal within trial
  sha: string;                           // 40-char
  message: string;
  filesChanged: number;
  insertions: number;
  deletions: number;
  diff: string;                          // patch text
}
```

**Big-diff tripwire:** the archive engine fails loud if any single diff
exceeds 10MB. Realistic diffs are <500KB; the cap is a "we'll figure
out blob storage if it ever bites" tripwire, not a constraint we expect
to hit.

### Standard probes

Three probes ship in MVP, registered with the laboratory plugin's probe
registry:

- **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — reads every book in the test guild,
  writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-dumps`. Summary
  is `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N, ... } }`.
- **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between the codex base
  and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to
  `lab-trial-codex-commits`. Summary is
  `{ headSha, commitCount, totalDiffBytes }`.
- **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig template name,
  framework SHA, resolved plugin pins, and a snapshot of the trial
  manifest. Summary-as-data: no bulk book; the summary itself is the
  captured data. Included in the default rig template; opt out by
  authoring a custom template.

### Trial-writ linkage

Just the foreign key. `lab-trial-archives.trialId` references
`clerk/writs.id`. Lookup is via FK query — there is no
`laboratory.archived-as` clerk link, because clerk links are
writ-to-writ and archive records are not writs.

`nsg writ show <trialId>` shows the trial writ with its config. To
surface the archive, run `nsg lab trial-show <trialId>`.

### Atomicity

Per-engine, not per-trial. The rig grafts probes ahead of the archive
engine; each probe writes its data atomically inside its own SQLite
transaction. The archive engine runs after all probes complete and
writes the `lab-trial-archives` index row atomically once. The index
row is the join key for queries — probe-book rows without a matching
archive-index row are orphan data from trials whose rigs failed before
reaching the archive step. Orphans are safe to ignore in queries
(every analytical query starts from `lab-trial-archives` and joins
out). Cleanup of orphan probe rows is future polish; MVP leaves them
in place.

The teardown engines (`lab.guild-teardown`, `lab.codex-teardown`)
refuse to run unless the trial's archive row exists.

### Probe registry and extraction-dispatch

Probes self-declare an extraction handler that materializes their
captured data to a directory. The extract tool dispatches via the
registry — no hardcoded probe knowledge in the tool itself. MVP
contract:

```ts
interface ProbeEngineDesign extends EngineDesign {
  // existing
  run(givens, context): Promise<EngineRunResult>;

  // new
  extract(args: {
    trialId: string;
    targetDir: string;        // probe writes outputs here (subdir)
    guild: GuildHandle;        // for reading the probe's books
  }): Promise<{
    files: Array<{ path: string; bytes: number }>;
  }>;
}
```

Tracked at click `c-momkil4p`. Each built-in probe ships its own
extractor:

- `lab.probe-stacks-dump` materializes to
  `<targetDir>/stacks-export/<plugin>-<book>.json` (one JSON array
  per source book).
- `lab.probe-git-range` materializes to
  `<targetDir>/codex-history/{commits-manifest.yaml,NNNN-<sha>.patch}`.
- `lab.probe-trial-context` materializes to
  `<targetDir>/trial-context.yaml`.

The extract tool composes per-probe outputs and additionally generates
`manifest.yaml` (from the trial writ's `ext.laboratory.config`) and
`README.md` (from archive metadata + probe summaries) at the top
level.

### CLI surface

- **`nsg lab trial-show <trialId>`** — print archive metadata + probe
  summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
- **`nsg lab trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]`** —
  materialize all captured data to a directory. Refuses to overwrite
  unless `--force`. Probe registry dispatches per-probe extractors.
- **`nsg lab trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format jsonl|json]`** —
  stream one source book for analysis pipelines. Default `jsonl`.

For programmatic analysis without going through extract: scripts can
attach the lab guild's stacks DB directly (DuckDB reads SQLite
natively) and query `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` / `lab-trial-codex-commits`
with JSON1 expressions.

### Annotations live sanctum-side

Analysis notes, findings, and human-authored interpretation of trial
data live in sanctum markdown that references `trialId` as the join
key — not in any laboratory book. The captured data in books is
immutable apparatus output; analysis is a sanctum activity. They were
never the same thing.

### Plugin packaging

For MVP, all three books and all three standard probes bundle in
`@shardworks/laboratory`. The code organizes per-probe
(`src/probes/<probe-name>/{engine.ts, book.ts, extractor.ts}`) and the
probe registry is built from per-probe registrations rather than a
hardcoded list. When a third-party probe forces the issue, lifting a
built-in probe into its own plugin (`@shardworks/lab-probe-stacks-dump`,
etc.) is a mechanical move — no architectural surgery. Tracked as a
parked v2 path; no click filed yet pending a forcing function.

## Status

Skeleton with stub engines. The trial writ type is registered; the rig
template, manifest CLI, and stub engines are wired end-to-end (smoke
tested against `vibers`). The remaining MVP work: real engine
implementations under click `c-moma9llq` — codex fixtures, guild
fixtures, scenario engines, probe engines (now including
`lab.probe-trial-context`), the archive engine, and the probe registry
+ extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`).

## Background

This package previously held a CDC-based observational stub (data
mirroring of writs/sessions into the sanctum). That instrument was
retired 2026-04-30 — its underlying signals (patron-set spec quality
ratings, structured commission review) had been hollowed out by the
shift to automated planning and static review pipelines. The package
was kept as a no-op so existing `guild.json` registrations stayed
loadable; the apparatus reshape reuses the package and the registered
plugin id, but the old data-mirroring code and types are gone.

1	/**
2	 * @shardworks/laboratory-apparatus — The Laboratory.
3	 *
4	 * Apparatus for running trial-shaped experiments on guild
5	 * configurations. Audiences:
6	 *
7	 *   - **Nexus dev:** cost/quality tuning (P3 work, prompt evaluation,
8	 *     plugin variant comparison) — automate the experimental-
9	 *     infrastructure spec at experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-
10	 *     artifacts.md.
11	 *   - **End users:** evaluate their own prompts/plugins/configs by
12	 *     authoring trial manifests against a stable apparatus surface.
13	 *
14	 * Architecture (MVP0):
15	 *
16	 *   - One writ type: `trial` — a single execution unit. Lifecycle
17	 *     mirrors mandate. (Higher-level `experiment` grouping is parked
18	 *     for v2; click c-momaacry.)
19	 *   - One canonical rig template: `post-and-collect-default` —
20	 *     composes fixture-setup, scenario, probes, teardown, and archive
21	 *     engines from the writ's `ext.laboratory.config`.
22	 *   - Engines: fixtures (setup/teardown, DAG), scenario (cross-guild
23	 *     commission post + wait), probes (data extraction), archive
24	 *     (still in design at click c-momaa5o9).
25	 *
26	 * The plugin is currently a skeleton — only the trial writ-type
27	 * registration is wired. Engine designs, rig templates, and CLI tools
28	 * are added as their respective implementation children land.
29	 *
30	 * See: experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md
31	 */
32	
33	import type { Plugin } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
34	import { guild } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
35	import type { ClerkApi } from '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus';
36	
37	// ── Public type re-exports ───────────────────────────────────────────
38	
39	export {
40	  TRIAL_TYPE_NAME,
41	  TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG,
42	} from './types.ts';
43	export type {
44	  LaboratoryConfig,
45	  LaboratoryTrialConfig,
46	  TrialFixtureDecl,
47	  TrialScenarioDecl,
48	  TrialProbeDecl,
49	  TrialArchiveDecl,
50	  TrialSlug,
51	} from './types.ts';
52	
53	// ── Apparatus ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	
55	import { TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG } from './types.ts';
56	import { engines } from './engines/index.ts';
57	import { rigTemplates, rigTemplateMappings } from './template.ts';
58	import { tools } from './tools/index.ts';
59	
60	const laboratoryPlugin: Plugin = {
61	  apparatus: {
62	    /**
63	     * Hard requires — apparatuses whose APIs the Laboratory's startup
64	     * code calls directly (clerk for writ-type registration), plus
65	     * those whose presence is structurally needed for the lab's
66	     * contributions to be useful (spider for rig-template execution,
67	     * fabricator for engine-design registration). Stacks is required
68	     * transitively via clerk.
69	     */
70	    requires: ['stacks', 'clerk', 'spider', 'fabricator'],
71	
72	    /**
73	     * Recommended at startup; required at engine-execution time when
74	     * the relevant fixture or scenario engine actually fires. Marking
75	     * them as recommends (not requires) lets a guild start the lab
76	     * without these installed — the trials that touch the missing
77	     * surface fail when their engine runs, not at startup.
78	     */
79	    recommends: ['codexes', 'animator'],
80	
81	    /**
82	     * Kit contributions wired up:
83	     *
84	     *   - `engines`              — every Laboratory engine design
85	     *                               (5 phase orchestrators + the
86	     *                               work-engine stubs that get
87	     *                               grafted by them). Stubs land
88	     *                               their real behavior under their
89	     *                               respective implementation clicks.
90	     *   - `rigTemplates`         — `post-and-collect-default` —
91	     *                               enumerates the five phase
92	     *                               orchestrators in sequence.
93	     *   - `rigTemplateMappings`  — `trial` → `post-and-collect-default`.
94	     *   - `tools`                — the manifest CLI surface
95	     *                               (`nsg lab trial-post`).
96	     */
97	    supportKit: {
98	      engines,
99	      rigTemplates,
100	      rigTemplateMappings,
101	      tools,
102	    },
103	
104	    async start() {
105	      // Register the `trial` writ type with the Clerk. The
106	      // registration window closes at framework `phase:started`;
107	      // calling later throws.
108	      const clerk = guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk');
109	      clerk.registerWritType(TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG);
110	    },
111	  },
112	};
113	
114	export default laboratoryPlugin;
115

1	/**
2	 * The Laboratory — public types.
3	 *
4	 * Two concerns live here:
5	 *
6	 *   1. The `trial` writ type configuration — its state machine, registered
7	 *      with the Clerk at startup. Mirrors the mandate lifecycle (`new →
8	 *      open → completed | failed | cancelled`, with `stuck` as a
9	 *      non-terminal "needs attention" state off `open`). Trials are leaves
10	 *      in v1 — the `experiment` parent type is reserved for v2 (see click
11	 *      c-momaacry).
12	 *
13	 *   2. The `ext.laboratory.config` schema — the structured trial
14	 *      configuration carried on every trial writ. Mirrors the YAML
15	 *      manifest shape exactly so the CLI's translation layer is a thin
16	 *      validator + post step.
17	 *
18	 * The legacy `LaboratoryConfig` (data-mirroring stub) is removed — that
19	 * plugin's no-op phase is over.
20	 *
21	 * See: experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md (the standalone-bash
22	 * spec the apparatus supersedes; its run.yaml shape is the template here).
23	 */
24	
25	import type { WritTypeConfig } from '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus';
26	
27	// ── Writ-type configuration ──────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	/** The writ-type name registered with the Clerk. */
30	export const TRIAL_TYPE_NAME = 'trial';
31	
32	/**
33	 * The `trial` writ type. State machine mirrors mandate's
34	 * `new → open → completed/failed/cancelled` with `stuck` as a
35	 * non-terminal off `open`. Children-cascade behavior is included so
36	 * trials can be nested under a future `experiment` parent without a
37	 * config-level migration; in v1 trials are leaves and the cascade
38	 * triggers are inert.
39	 */
40	export const TRIAL_WRIT_TYPE_CONFIG: WritTypeConfig = {
41	  name: TRIAL_TYPE_NAME,
42	  states: [
43	    {
44	      name: 'new',
45	      classification: 'initial',
46	      allowedTransitions: ['open', 'cancelled'],
47	    },
48	    {
49	      name: 'open',
50	      classification: 'active',
51	      allowedTransitions: ['stuck', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled'],
52	    },
53	    {
54	      name: 'stuck',
55	      classification: 'active',
56	      attrs: ['stuck'],
57	      allowedTransitions: ['open', 'failed', 'cancelled'],
58	    },
59	    {
60	      name: 'completed',
61	      classification: 'terminal',
62	      attrs: ['success'],
63	      allowedTransitions: [],
64	    },
65	    {
66	      name: 'failed',
67	      classification: 'terminal',
68	      attrs: ['failure'],
69	      allowedTransitions: [],
70	    },
71	    {
72	      name: 'cancelled',
73	      classification: 'terminal',
74	      attrs: ['cancelled'],
75	      allowedTransitions: [],
76	    },
77	  ],
78	  childrenBehavior: {
79	    allSuccess: { transition: 'completed', copyResolution: true },
80	    anyFailure: { transition: 'failed', copyResolution: true },
81	    parentTerminal: {
82	      transition: 'cancelled',
83	      resolution: 'Automatically cancelled due to parent termination',
84	    },
85	  },
86	};
87	
88	// ── ext.laboratory.config schema ─────────────────────────────────────
89	//
90	// The structured trial configuration. Mirrors the YAML manifest shape
91	// (see docs/laboratory/manifest-format.md once authored). Every field
92	// here will be validated by the manifest CLI before the trial writ is
93	// posted.
94	
95	/**
96	 * Slug carried on every trial — used in disposable-surface naming
97	 * (codex repo, guild dir). Authored by the manifest. Length-limited at
98	 * the CLI boundary (see manifest-cli child); engines treat it as
99	 * already-validated input.
100	 *
101	 * Format target: lowercase kebab-case, alphanumeric + hyphen only,
102	 * 1–40 chars. Disposable-resource names of the shape
103	 * `trial-<slug>-<trialId-prefix>` slot below 63-char DNS / GH-repo
104	 * limits with margin.
105	 */
106	export type TrialSlug = string;
107	
108	/**
109	 * One fixture declaration in a trial. Fixtures handle setup and
110	 * teardown of disposable surfaces (codex repos, test guilds, etc.)
111	 * and form a dependency DAG.
112	 *
113	 * Setup and teardown are paired engines. By convention the teardown
114	 * engine id is the setup id with `-setup` swapped for `-teardown`
115	 * (e.g. `lab.codex-setup` ↔ `lab.codex-teardown`). The `teardownEngineId`
116	 * field overrides this default when the convention doesn't fit.
117	 *
118	 * MVP0 ships trial-scope only — every fixture is fresh per trial,
119	 * torn down at trial end. The `scope` and `mutability` hooks are
120	 * reserved for v2 (see click c-mom19282).
121	 */
122	export interface TrialFixtureDecl {
123	  /** Fixture id within this trial. Referenced by `dependsOn`. */
124	  id: string;
125	  /** Setup engine design id (e.g. `'lab.codex-setup'`). */
126	  engineId: string;
127	  /** Override teardown engine id. Defaults to convention (`-setup` → `-teardown`). */
128	  teardownEngineId?: string;
129	  /** Givens passed to BOTH the setup and teardown engines. */
130	  givens: Record<string, unknown>;
131	  /** Other fixture ids that must be set up before this one. */
132	  dependsOn?: string[];
133	  /** Reserved for v2 — only `'trial'` valid in v1. */
134	  scope?: 'trial';
135	  /** Reserved for v2 — informational in v1. */
136	  mutability?: 'mutable' | 'read-only' | 'snapshotted';
137	}
138	
139	/**
140	 * The scenario — what work runs inside the trial after fixtures are
141	 * up. In v1, scenarios are themselves engine designs (commission-post-
142	 * xguild + wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild are the canonical pair).
143	 * Future scenarios may compose multiple engine invocations.
144	 */
145	export interface TrialScenarioDecl {
146	  /** Engine design id to run as the scenario. */
147	  engineId: string;
148	  /** Givens passed to the scenario engine. */
149	  givens: Record<string, unknown>;
150	}
151	
152	/** A probe — extracts data from one or more fixtures. */
153	export interface TrialProbeDecl {
154	  /** Probe id within this trial. */
155	  id: string;
156	  /** Engine design id (e.g. `'lab.probe-stacks-dump'`). */
157	  engineId: string;
158	  /** Givens passed to the probe engine. */
159	  givens: Record<string, unknown>;
160	}
161	
162	/**
163	 * The archive declaration — where probe outputs land at trial end.
164	 * The engine and storage layout are being designed under click
165	 * c-momaa5o9; this shape is provisional.
166	 */
167	export interface TrialArchiveDecl {
168	  /** Engine design id (e.g. `'lab.archive'`). */
169	  engineId: string;
170	  /** Givens passed to the archive engine. */
171	  givens: Record<string, unknown>;
172	}
173	
174	/**
175	 * The structured payload carried on every trial writ at
176	 * `ext.laboratory.config`. The manifest YAML on disk has the same
177	 * shape plus a top-level `description` field that lands on the writ
178	 * body instead of in `ext`.
179	 */
180	export interface LaboratoryTrialConfig {
181	  /** Trial slug (used in disposable-surface naming). */
182	  slug: TrialSlug;
183	  /** Fixtures to set up (DAG; topo-sort happens at template time). */
184	  fixtures: TrialFixtureDecl[];
185	  /** The workload that runs after setup. */
186	  scenario: TrialScenarioDecl;
187	  /** Probes to extract data. Run after the scenario reaches its end-condition. */
188	  probes: TrialProbeDecl[];
189	  /** Archive — where probe outputs go. */
190	  archive: TrialArchiveDecl;
191	}
192	
193	// ── GuildConfig augmentation ─────────────────────────────────────────
194	//
195	// The Laboratory contributes no top-level guild.json config in v1.
196	// Trial-specific configuration lives on individual writs at
197	// `ext.laboratory.config`, not in guild config. Reserved for future
198	// global lab settings (e.g. default codex org, archive root path).
199	
200	export interface LaboratoryConfig {
201	  /**
202	   * Reserved for v2. No fields read in v1 — present so guild.json
203	   * fragments that include an empty `laboratory: {}` block still
204	   * typecheck.
205	   */
206	  reserved?: never;
207	}
208	
209	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
210	  interface GuildConfig {
211	    laboratory?: LaboratoryConfig;
212	  }
213	}
214

1	/**
2	 * Engine bag — every engine the Laboratory contributes via supportKit.
3	 *
4	 * Keys are the engine design ids the Spider's template references.
5	 * The Fabricator scans this object at startup and registers each
6	 * design under the laboratory plugin id.
7	 *
8	 * The five phase orchestrators (`lab.{setup,scenario,probes,archive,
9	 * teardown}-phase`) form the rig template's static backbone; they
10	 * read `writ.ext.laboratory.config` at run time and emit per-phase
11	 * grafts. The other engines (codex/guild fixtures, scenario,
12	 * probes, archive) are the work engines those grafts reference.
13	 *
14	 * Stubs land their real implementations under their respective
15	 * implementation children.
16	 */
17	
18	import type { EngineDesign } from '@shardworks/fabricator-apparatus';
19	import { phaseEngines } from './phases.ts';
20	import { codexSetupEngine, codexTeardownEngine } from './codex-fixture.ts';
21	import { guildSetupEngine, guildTeardownEngine } from './guild-fixture.ts';
22	import {
23	  commissionPostXguildEngine,
24	  waitForWritTerminalXguildEngine,
25	} from './scenario-xguild.ts';
26	import {
27	  archiveStub,
28	  probeGitRangeStub,
29	  probeStacksDumpStub,
30	} from './stubs.ts';
31	
32	export const engines: Record<string, EngineDesign> = {
33	  // Phase orchestrators (template backbone)
34	  ...phaseEngines,
35	
36	  // Work engines (graft targets)
37	  'lab.codex-setup': codexSetupEngine,
38	  'lab.codex-teardown': codexTeardownEngine,
39	  'lab.guild-setup': guildSetupEngine,
40	  'lab.guild-teardown': guildTeardownEngine,
41	  'lab.commission-post-xguild': commissionPostXguildEngine,
42	  'lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild': waitForWritTerminalXguildEngine,
43	  'lab.probe-stacks-dump': probeStacksDumpStub,
44	  'lab.probe-git-range': probeGitRangeStub,
45	  'lab.archive': archiveStub,
46	};
47

1	/**
2	 * Skeleton (stub) engine implementations — for engines that have not yet
3	 * been replaced by real code. Stubs log, yield a placeholder payload,
4	 * and return `{ status: 'completed' }` so the rig template's wiring
5	 * stays exercised end-to-end while implementation lands incrementally.
6	 *
7	 * Remaining stubs and the click that lands their real implementation:
8	 *
9	 *   - lab.probe-stacks-dump
10	 *     lab.probe-git-range                            — c-momaa3w7
11	 *   - lab.archive                                    — c-momaa5o9
12	 *
13	 * When swapping a stub for its real implementation, move it to its own
14	 * file under `engines/`; `stubs.ts` shrinks toward empty.
15	 *
16	 * Already replaced:
17	 *   - lab.codex-setup / lab.codex-teardown          (c-moma9y1k)
18	 *     → see `engines/codex-fixture.ts`.
19	 *   - lab.guild-setup / lab.guild-teardown          (c-momaa03d)
20	 *     → see `engines/guild-fixture.ts`.
21	 *   - lab.commission-post-xguild
22	 *     lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild             (c-momaa1vt)
23	 *     → see `engines/scenario-xguild.ts`.
24	 */
25	
26	import type { EngineDesign, EngineRunResult } from '@shardworks/fabricator-apparatus';
27	
28	/**
29	 * Build a stub engine: yields a placeholder, returns completed. No
30	 * console output — a daemon running concurrent rigs would otherwise
31	 * see stub chatter dominate its logs.
32	 */
33	function stubEngine(designId: string, summary: string): EngineDesign {
34	  return {
35	    id: designId,
36	    async run(givens): Promise<EngineRunResult> {
37	      return {
38	        status: 'completed',
39	        yields: {
40	          stub: true,
41	          designId,
42	          summary,
43	          // Echo a minimal subset of givens so downstream stubs can
44	          // resolve `${yields.<id>.<key>}` templates without crashing.
45	          givenKeys: Object.keys(givens),
46	        },
47	      };
48	    },
49	  };
50	}
51	
52	// ── Fixture engines ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
53	
54	// lab.codex-setup / lab.codex-teardown moved to codex-fixture.ts.
55	// lab.guild-setup / lab.guild-teardown moved to guild-fixture.ts.
56	
57	// ── Scenario engines ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
58	
59	// lab.commission-post-xguild / lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild moved
60	// to scenario-xguild.ts.
61	
62	// ── Probe engines ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
63	
64	export const probeStacksDumpStub = stubEngine(
65	  'lab.probe-stacks-dump',
66	  'export every book in test guild as JSON-per-table with carve-out for >50KB rows',
67	);
68	
69	export const probeGitRangeStub = stubEngine(
70	  'lab.probe-git-range',
71	  'capture per-commit diffs + commits-manifest.yaml between base and head SHAs',
72	);
73	
74	// ── Archive engine ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	export const archiveStub = stubEngine(
77	  'lab.archive',
78	  'archive probe outputs to the configured target (open: lab books vs sanctum vs hybrid; c-momaa5o9)',
79	);

1	/**
2	 * The Laboratory's rig templates.
3	 *
4	 * MVP0 ships ONE template: `post-and-collect-default`. Its engine
5	 * list IS the trial-flow backbone — five phase orchestrators
6	 * (setup → scenario → probes → archive → teardown) wired in
7	 * sequence. Each phase orchestrator is a clockwork engine: it
8	 * reads `writ.ext.laboratory.config`, computes the per-phase
9	 * graft, and returns immediately. The grafted engines do the
10	 * real work; the template documents the flow.
11	 *
12	 * Why the backbone is in the template (not in a single
13	 * orchestrator):
14	 *
15	 *   - The template is the user-facing shape document for a trial.
16	 *     With phases visible, reading the template tells you "this
17	 *     rig runs setup, then scenario, then probes, then archive,
18	 *     then teardown."
19	 *   - Per-phase failure visibility: oculus and introspection tools
20	 *     see the phase backbone in the rig's engine list; "the probes
21	 *     phase failed" is a more useful signal than "the orchestrator
22	 *     failed somewhere."
23	 *   - Each phase orchestrator implementation is cohesive (DAG
24	 *     sort, parallel scatter, reverse-sequential chain) — splitting
25	 *     them avoids one big function with phase-mode flags.
26	 *   - Sets up an extension point: a future plugin contributing
27	 *     `lab.warmup-phase` (or similar) could slot into a custom
28	 *     template alongside the existing phases.
29	 *
30	 * Cross-phase data flow happens at the work-engine layer, not the
31	 * orchestrator layer. All grafted engines share a single namespace,
32	 * so e.g. the scenario engine's upstream points directly at each
33	 * fixture-setup engine — phase orchestrators don't need to relay
34	 * yields between phases.
35	 *
36	 * Rig template name vs writ-type mapping: the Spider qualifies
37	 * kit-contributed template keys by plugin id when storing them in
38	 * its registry. The unqualified key `'post-and-collect-default'`
39	 * registers as `'laboratory.post-and-collect-default'`. The mapping
40	 * value below uses the unqualified form because spider.ts joins
41	 * the plugin id when resolving mappings (matches astrolabe's
42	 * convention).
43	 */
44	
45	import type { RigTemplate } from '@shardworks/spider-apparatus';
46	
47	/**
48	 * The post-and-collect-default rig template — five phase
49	 * orchestrators in sequence.
50	 *
51	 * Each phase orchestrator reads the trial's `ext.laboratory.config`,
52	 * computes its phase's graft, and returns. graftTail is set only
53	 * on `lab.teardown-phase` (the rig's true end) — intermediate
54	 * phases let the next phase fire immediately, since real
55	 * work-engines wait on explicit upstream refs.
56	 */
57	export const POST_AND_COLLECT_DEFAULT: RigTemplate = {
58	  engines: [
59	    {
60	      id: 'setup-phase',
61	      designId: 'lab.setup-phase',
62	      // Head engine — no upstream.
63	      givens: { writ: '${writ}' },
64	    },
65	    {
66	      id: 'scenario-phase',
67	      designId: 'lab.scenario-phase',
68	      upstream: ['setup-phase'],
69	      givens: { writ: '${writ}' },
70	    },
71	    {
72	      id: 'probes-phase',
73	      designId: 'lab.probes-phase',
74	      upstream: ['scenario-phase'],
75	      givens: { writ: '${writ}' },
76	    },
77	    {
78	      id: 'archive-phase',
79	      designId: 'lab.archive-phase',
80	      upstream: ['probes-phase'],
81	      givens: { writ: '${writ}' },
82	    },
83	    {
84	      id: 'teardown-phase',
85	      designId: 'lab.teardown-phase',
86	      upstream: ['archive-phase'],
87	      givens: { writ: '${writ}' },
88	    },
89	  ],
90	  // resolutionEngine intentionally unset — the Spider falls back
91	  //   to the last completed engine, which is the final teardown
92	  //   (or archive when there are no fixtures), via the
93	  //   teardown-phase orchestrator's graftTail.
94	};
95	
96	/** The Laboratory's rig templates, keyed by unqualified template name. */
97	export const rigTemplates: Record<string, RigTemplate> = {
98	  'post-and-collect-default': POST_AND_COLLECT_DEFAULT,
99	};
100	
101	/** Writ-type → unqualified-template-name mappings. */
102	export const rigTemplateMappings: Record<string, string> = {
103	  trial: 'post-and-collect-default',
104	};
105

1	/**
2	 * Tool bag — every CLI tool the Laboratory contributes.
3	 *
4	 * The framework auto-discovers `supportKit.tools` and registers each
5	 * with the Instrumentarium for CLI dispatch. Hyphen-prefix grouping
6	 * gives `nsg lab <subcommand>` for tools named `lab-<subcommand>`.
7	 */
8	
9	import trialPost from './trial-post.ts';
10	
11	// Untyped array: `ToolDefinition` is generic over the schema shape and
12	// each tool's handler narrows the params type — typing the array as
13	// `ToolDefinition[]` would force erasure to `Record<string, unknown>`.
14	// The Instrumentarium scans the array structurally; no array-level
15	// type annotation is needed (matches clerk's `tools:` precedent).
16	export const tools = [trialPost];
17

1	{
2	  "name": "@shardworks/laboratory-apparatus",
3	  "version": "0.0.0",
4	  "private": true,
5	  "license": "ISC",
6	  "repository": {
7	    "type": "git",
8	    "url": "https://github.com/shardworks/nexus-mk2",
9	    "directory": "packages/laboratory"
10	  },
11	  "description": "The Laboratory — apparatus for running trial-shaped experiments on guild configurations (cost/quality tuning, prompt evaluation, plugin variant comparison)",
12	  "type": "module",
13	  "exports": {
14	    ".": "./src/index.ts"
15	  },

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * @shardworks/stacks-apparatus — The Stacks apparatus.
3	 *
4	 * Guild persistence layer: NoSQL document store with CDC, transactions,
5	 * and swappable backend. Default export is the apparatus plugin.
6	 *
7	 * See: docs/specification.md
8	 */
9	
10	import { createStacksApparatus } from './stacks.ts';
11	
12	// ── Public types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13	
14	export type {
15	  // Plugin config
16	  StacksConfig,
17	
18	  // Document model
19	  BookEntry,
20	  BookSchema,
21	
22	  // Book handles
23	  Book,
24	  ReadOnlyBook,
25	
26	  // Query language
27	  Scalar,
28	  WhereCondition,
29	  WhereClause,
30	  OrderEntry,
31	  OrderBy,
32	  Pagination,
33	  BookQuery,
34	  ListOptions,
35	
36	  // CDC
37	  ChangeEvent,
38	  CreateEvent,
39	  UpdateEvent,
40	  DeleteEvent,
41	  ChangeHandler,
42	  WatchOptions,
43	
44	  // API
45	  StacksApi,
46	  TransactionContext,
47	} from './types.ts';
48	
49	// ── Backend types (for alternative implementations) ───────────────────
50	
51	export type {
52	  StacksBackend,
53	  BackendTransaction,
54	  BackendOptions,
55	  BookRef,
56	  InternalQuery,
57	  InternalCondition,
58	  CountQuery,
59	  PutResult,
60	  PatchResult,
61	  DeleteResult,
62	} from './backend.ts';
63	
64	// ── Apparatus factory ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	export { createStacksApparatus } from './stacks.ts';
67	
68	// ── Default export: the apparatus plugin ──────────────────────────────
69	
70	export default createStacksApparatus();
71

567	   */
568	  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
569

571	   * Show a writ by id. Throws if not found.
572	   */
